heroSubtitle: Turn portfolio risk into a stat line sports fans already know how to read.

blocks.heading: Our Strategic Position
blocks.subheading: ShotClock is an AI risk briefing built on the cTrader MCP server for casual, gamified investors who follow sports more closely than markets. It reads your live positions each morning and reports them back as a basketball box score: shot clock counting down to margin pressure, fouls for risk rule breaks, a daily stat line for P&L. The goal is not more data. It is data a sports fan already knows how to feel urgency about.

items.title: Audience
items.description: Casual gamified investors, sports-fan crossover

items.title: Foundation
items.description: Official cTrader MCP server

items.title: Format
items.description: Daily AI-narrated briefing

blocks.heading: Our Competitive Sweet Spot
blocks.subheading: We sit at the intersection of three things nobody else combines.

items.title: AI-agent trading access
items.description: Live position and exposure data via cTrader MCP, no manual entry

items.title: Sports literacy
items.description: Basketball stats are a shared language for urgency, discipline, and streaks

items.title: Casual, not professional
items.description: Built for people who check ESPN before they check margin, not day traders

blocks.heading: Why This Strategy?
blocks.subheading: Three reasons this is worth building

items.title: Foundation
items.description:
The cTrader MCP server already exposes live account, position, and order data to an AI agent. The hard integration problem is solved. What is missing is a reason for a casual user to open the app every day.

Risk data exists. Nobody has made it fun to check.

items.title: Vision
items.description:
Risk management fails for casual traders not because the numbers are hidden, but because the numbers are boring. A margin ratio does not create urgency. A shot clock does.

We are not simplifying risk. We are translating it into a language the audience already feels instinctively.

items.title: The Opportunity
items.description:
Fantasy sports proved that turning a passive interest into a daily stat-checking habit works at massive scale. Nobody has applied that mechanic to personal risk management.

The demo-account, sports-fan crossover is underserved because most trading tools are built by and for professional traders.

items.title: Trade execution
items.description: We stay a briefing and companion layer on top of cTrader. Placing or managing trades happens in cTrader itself, not in our app.

items.title: Professional or institutional traders
items.description: That audience wants precision instrumentation, not a scoreboard metaphor. We are not building for them and will not add complexity to chase them.

items.title: Sports outside basketball at launch
items.description: One sport metaphor, done well, beats three done shallowly. Other sports become a later personalization layer, not a v1 feature.

blocks.heading: Evolutionary Strategy
blocks.subheading: From daily briefing to social league

items.title: Box Score MVP
items.description: AI agent pulls live positions and exposure from cTrader MCP and generates one daily basketball-style risk briefing per user

items.title: Foul Trouble Alerts
items.description: Real-time nudges when risk rules are broken, framed as foul calls, plus a running discipline streak

items.title: Personal Stat History
items.description: Season-style stat tracking so users see their risk discipline trend over weeks, not just today

items.title: League Layer
items.description: Friends compare stat lines and leaderboards ranked on discipline, not raw returns

items.title: Commentator Voice Options
items.description: Personalization of the AI narration style, from hype announcer to calm coach

blocks.heading: Our Reasoning
blocks.subheading: The logic behind the bets above

items.title: Why basketball specifically
items.description: Basketball has a built-in urgency mechanic, the shot clock, that maps directly onto time-pressured risk decisions. Few other sports have as clean a metaphor for a countdown.

items.title: Why casual over professional
items.description: Professional traders already have risk tooling and do not want a game layered on top of it. Casual traders have neither the tooling nor the motivation. That gap is the opening.

items.title: Why briefing over full app
items.description: A daily push-style briefing is a smaller build, ships faster, and tests the core hypothesis, that gamified framing drives habit, before we invest in a heavier product.

items.title: Daily briefing generator
items.description: AI agent connects to cTrader MCP, reads positions and exposure, and produces one basketball-framed briefing per user per day.

items.title: Risk-to-stat mapping engine
items.description: A defined, explainable mapping from real risk metrics like margin level and drawdown to shot clock, fouls, and box score values, so the metaphor stays honest.

items.title: Streak and achievement tracking
items.description: Lightweight state tracking discipline over time, the foundation for the later league and leaderboard layer.

items.title: Marcus
items.subtitle: Weekend dabbler, fantasy basketball player, demo cTrader account
items.description: Marcus opens three fantasy sports apps before he opens his trading account. He trades small positions on a demo account for fun, checks scores obsessively, but forgets his open positions exist until something goes wrong.

children.title: Current Behavior
children.description: Opens a position, gets distracted by a game, checks the account days later when a notification finally forces him to

children.title: Finds margin and exposure numbers boring and hard to parse
Has no daily reason to check risk before it becomes urgent
Quote
children.description: I check my fantasy team's box score every morning. My trading account, never.

jobs.title: Understand my risk without reading a finance manual
pains: No urgency until it is too late
gains: A stat line he already knows how to read
A reason to check in daily

items.title: Priya
items.subtitle: League commissioner type, plays fantasy sports competitively with a friend group
items.description: Priya runs the group chat for her fantasy league and loves anything with a leaderboard. She trades a small demo account mostly because her friends do, and she is more motivated by beating them than by the trade itself.

children.title: Current Behavior
children.description: Compares fantasy stats with friends daily, has never compared trading performance because there is no fun way to do it

children.title: No social layer exists for casual trading discipline
Winning trades feels good but risky trades feel invisible
Quote
children.description: If there was a leaderboard for not blowing up your account, I would be at the top of it.

jobs.title: Show my friends I trade with discipline, not just luck
pains: No way to prove good risk behavior socially
gains: Bragging rights that reward the right behavior

blocks.heading: North Star: Risk Briefings Opened Per Day
blocks.subheading: This is the single number that proves the core bet: that gamified risk framing gets checked daily. At launch, a handful of beta users opening most of their daily briefings is success. Meaningful traction looks like thousands of daily opens with a majority of active users opening their briefing most days of the week.

items.title: Launch baseline
items.description: 20 to 50 beta users, 60 percent daily open rate

items.title: Meaningful traction
items.description: 2,000+ briefings opened per day across the user base

blocks.heading: Value Delivery Metrics
blocks.subheading: Signals that the product is genuinely helping, not just entertaining

items.title: Engagement
items.subtitle: Daily briefing open rate
items.description: Target 60 percent of active users open their briefing on a given day, badge shows habit strength

items.title: Discipline
items.subtitle: Foul-free streak length
items.description: Average consecutive days without a risk rule violation, target trending upward month over month

items.title: Comprehension
items.subtitle: Post-briefing risk action rate
items.description: Percent of users who adjust a position within 1 hour of an alert briefing, target 25 percent

items.title: Retention
items.subtitle: Week 4 active users
items.description: Percent of signed-up users still opening briefings after 4 weeks, target 35 percent

items.title: Satisfaction
items.subtitle: Briefing usefulness rating
items.description: In-app 1 tap rating after each briefing, target average above 4 out of 5

blocks.heading: Business Health Metrics
blocks.subheading: Growth and sustainability signals at seed stage

items.title: Growth
items.subtitle: New connected accounts per week
items.description: Target 50 per week during beta, primary growth channel is friend referral within leagues

items.title: Activation
items.subtitle: cTrader MCP connection completion rate
items.description: Percent of signups who successfully connect a demo account, target 80 percent

items.title: Virality
items.subtitle: League invites sent per active user
items.description: Target 1.5 invites per user per month once the league layer ships

items.title: Cost efficiency
items.subtitle: AI briefing generation cost per user per month
items.description: Track to keep unit economics viable before any monetization layer is added

items.title: Churn
items.subtitle: Monthly briefing unsubscribe rate
items.description: Target under 8 percent monthly, early warning signal for metaphor fatigue
